name: Advocu MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Advocu — a platform for developer advocates to track their community activities, speaking engagements, and advocacy work. Enables AI agents to log activities, track conference talks and CFP submissions, manage community contributions, and generate advocacy reports through the Advocu API.
use_cases: Logging developer advocacy activities and speaking engagements from tracking agents
Managing CFP submissions and conference speaking history from DevRel agents
Generating community contribution reports from reporting agents
Tracking developer advocacy program metrics from program management agents
not_for: Teams not using Advocu platform for developer advocacy tracking
best_when: A developer advocate using Advocu wants AI agents to log activities, track speaking engagements, and manage their advocacy portfolio — automating routine advocacy tracking and reporting.
avoid_when: You don't use Advocu. Very niche tool for developer advocates — limited to Advocu platform users. Community tool by carlosazaustre (a well-known Spanish developer advocate).

auth.notes: Advocu API key required. Generate in Advocu settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Advocu has free tier for developer advocates
pricing.notes: Advocu free tier available. MCP server is free open source by carlosazaustre.

known_agent_gotchas: Very niche audience — only useful for developer advocates specifically using Advocu
carlosazaustre is a well-known DevRel figure — quality likely solid but audience is small
Advocu platform stability depends on startup longevity — evaluate platform risk
Community tool — not official Advocu integration
agent_readiness.security_notes: Advocu API key. HTTPS. Advocacy activity data stored on Advocu platform. Low risk for typical DevRel data.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth. HTTPS. DevRel activity data — low sensitivity.
